items.title: Wall Street set for lower open after Trump's comments dent Iran resolution hopes
items.summary_auto: Wall Street's main indexes were set for a lower open on Thursday, in the last session of a holiday-truncated week, after President ‌Donald Trump signaled more aggressive attacks on Iran, dampening expectations for a swift end to the Middle East conflict.

items.title: Bogus maple syrup from Quebec producer found on grocery store shelves
Family of mother killed in Alberta 'clinging' to hope her missing baby will be found
items.summary_auto: Ayla Egotik-Learn, a 23-year-old Inuk mother from Cambridge Bay, Nunavut, was discovered dead in January, inside her apartment northwest of Edmonton. Her baby has never been found and is presumed dead by RCMP.

items.title: Victoire clinch playoff spot, set PWHL point streak record in win over Goldeneyes
items.summary_auto: Hayley Scamurra scored twice and Ann-Renee Desbiens made 18 saves as the Montreal Victoire blanked the Vancouver Goldeneyes 3-0 on Wednesday night, clinching a playoff spot and extending their point streak to a league-record 12 games.

items.title: Death of near-blind refugee — left at closed Tim Hortons by U.S. Border Patrol — ruled a homicide
items.summary_auto: The death of a nearly blind refugee from Myanmar who was found on a Buffalo, N.Y., street in February — five days after U.S. Border Patrol agents left him outside a Tim Hortons — has been ruled a homicide.

items.title: New Fort St. John doctors to take thousands off waiting list
items.summary_auto: Dr. Manraj Khangura and Dr. Haroon Ahmed are international medical graduates finishing up their two years of residency training in the city. They will take on 3,000 new patients starting in September.

items.title: If court halts separatists' referendum bid, they'll push Danielle Smith to approve it anyway
items.summary_auto: A First Nation's injunction request, to be heard April 7, claims the independence petition drive threatens treaty rights.

items.title: Poilievre calls for federal tax holiday on fuel as gas prices spike
items.summary_auto: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is calling on the government to temporarily pause federal taxes on gas and diesel for the remainder of the year.

items.title: To meet next NATO spending target, Canada needs a credible fiscal plan, think-tank says
items.summary_auto: After hitting NATO's two per cent GDP spending target in March 2026, Canada now faces a daunting climb to five per cent by 2035, according to the CD Howe Institute, one of the country’s leading economic think-tanks.

items.title: Workers quit emergency care for Nova Scotia’s most vulnerable after CRA audit
items.summary_auto: A private company that’s received more than $184 million in public funds over the past eight years to care for some of Nova Scotia’s most vulnerable people is now losing workers so quickly, remaining workers say it’s a major safety issue.

items.title: War disrupts Irving Oil’s longtime Saudi crude supply
items.summary_auto: New Brunswick-based Irving Oil is looking for new supply options as the U.S. war on Iran threatens one of its most reliable, and long-standing, sources of crude oil.

items.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake off Indonesia topples buildings, kills 1 person
items.summary_auto: An undersea magnitude 7.4 earthquake toppled buildings in parts of northern Indonesia, sent people fleeing from their homes, killed at least one person and generated a small tsunami Thursday.

items.title: 25 men are sleeping on cots in the gymnasium of N.L.'s largest prison for the 'foreseeable future'
items.summary_auto: An inmate at Her Majesty's Penitentiary told a judge last week that he's dealing with a leaking roof and a rodent infestation while sleeping on a cot in the jail's gymnasium. The judge toured the facility, and said the gym is "not a very nice place to be living."

items.title: How a Montreal-based security firm stands to cash in on U.S. immigration crackdown
items.summary_auto: The Montreal-based security firm GardaWorld, backed by Quebec public financing, is now profiting from the expansion of U.S. immigration detention, raising questions about oversight and corporate accountability.

items.title: Why mysterious vegetables have been showing up, unordered, on some Ottawans' doorsteps
items.summary_auto: People in Ottawa have been receiving multiple deliveries of vegetables from Walmart that they never asked for. To add further confusion, the deliveries contain only one or two veggies. Giacomo Panico unpacks the mystery.

items.title: Scammers are targeting Canadians with a milk settlement that doesn't exist
items.summary_auto: At the same time that a phishing scam related to Canada's bread price-fixing settlement is going around, CBC News has learned people are being targeted by a similar scam involving dairy.

items.title: What Trump's prime-time TV pitch means for war on Iran
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump is telling Americans that while his objectives for the war against Iran are nearly achieved, he intends to escalate military operations for at least another two weeks.

items.title: What you need to know about the Artemis II moon mission
items.summary_auto: It's an exciting time as four intrepid astronauts — Canadian Space Agency's Jeremy Hansen, along with NASA astronauts, Christina Koch, Reid Wiseman and Victor Glover — are heading to the moon on a 10-day mission. Here's just some of what to expect over the next week.

items.title: Ottawa to spend $15M to help build 2 mental health treatment centres for first responders
items.summary_auto: The federal government will contribute $15 million to the construction of two new treatment facilities in Greater Toronto Area for first responders who are dealing with post-traumatic stress injuries.

items.title: Health Canada slaps new conditions on plasma donation company Grifols, still reviewing 2 Winnipeg deaths
items.summary_auto: Health Canada has slapped new terms and conditions on all of a company's paid plasma centres after multiple failed inspections where the regulator found "recurring, systemic deficiencies."

items.title: Proposed political neutrality legislation offensive to Alberta teachers, association says
items.summary_auto: The Alberta Teachers’ Association says the provincial government's suggestion that educators don't act with integrity or present issues in a balanced way is offensive.

items.title: Alberta to compel employers hiring temporary foreign workers to register provincially
items.summary_auto: Alberta's government is proposing changes to give it more oversight of who hires temporary foreign workers.

items.title: Watch as Artemis II departs Earth
items.summary_auto: NASA’s Artemis II mission will send the astronauts around the moon and back, farther from Earth than any previous human spaceflight. Among the crew is Canadian Jeremy Hansen who is set to become the first Canadian to reach deep space.

items.title: Trump says 'core strategic objectives' in Iran are near completion, without providing precise timeline
items.summary_auto: U.S. President ​Donald Trump said on ​Wednesday evening that ⁠Iran ⁠has ‌been decimated and that  the ⁠hard part of ‌the war is done, telling Americans that his "core strategic objectives" are near completion and that the war will end soon.

items.title: Deaths of 5 homeless Montrealers in 7 months prompt Quebec coroner inquiry
items.summary_auto: Quebec coroner Stéphanie Gamache will lead a public inquiry into the deaths of five unhoused people, which took place over the past seven months in Montreal. The city’s mayor, who was in tears in late March while announcing the deaths of two people experiencing homelessness, says the final report will include recommendations for authorities aimed at preventing such occurrences.

items.title: Union says no snow crab will be processed in N.L. until 'fair' price agreed upon
items.summary_auto: The union representing fishery workers in Newfoundland and Labrador says there will be no snow crab processed in the province until they get a deal for a "fair" price.

items.title: Federal 30-days-or-free policy for passports now in place
items.summary_auto: The federal government's new "30 days or free" policy for issuing passports takes effect on Wednesday.

items.title: Investigators link serial killer Ted Bundy to 1974 Utah murder
items.summary_auto: Utah officials announced on Wednesday that they were closing a five-decade-old case after finding "definitive proof" that American serial killer Ted Bundy  killed a 17-year-old Utah girl.

items.title: Conversion therapy survivors on edge as U.S. Supreme Court strikes down ban
items.summary_auto: The U.S. Supreme Court has ruled that banning conversion therapy for minors is a violation of free speech. But a man who once underwent the practice himself says it's about protecting vulnerable young people from serious — and sometimes fatal — harm.

items.title: Liberals planned to buy back 136,000 banned guns. Fewer than half that many were declared
items.summary_auto: The federal public safety minister's office says more than 67,000 assault-style firearms have been declared, far short of the 136,000 the government had budgeted for. The uptake combined with hurdles for the next stage are raising questions about whether the program will accomplish the government's goal of abolishing assault-style firearms in Canada.

items.title: Toronto police ramp up patrols at Jewish places of worship during Passover
items.summary_auto: The Jewish holiday of Passover begins Wednesday evening and is observed until next Thursday. Police in the Greater Toronto Area are proactively ramping up patrols around Jewish places of worship and community gathering spaces as Premier Doug Ford says many Jewish people report feeling unsafe.

items.title: Ontario misses federal funding deadline for nurse practitioners
items.summary_auto: Ontario's health minister shut down one avenue of publicly funding all nurse practitioners Wednesday as the province missed a federal deadline for ensuring their medically necessary services are covered.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | Launch of Artemis II mission
items.summary_auto: The Artemis II SLS rocket with the Orion capsule and its crew blasted off from the Kennedy Space Center in Cape Canaveral, Fla., on Wednesday on a nearly 10-day mission that's expected to take them around the moon and back to Earth.

items.title: Italy missed the World Cup again — but that's good news for Canada
items.summary_auto: CBC Sports' daily newsletter breaks down Italy's stunning loss in World Cup qualifying and how it could help co-host Canada.

items.title: Megan Thee Stallion takes 2 Broadway shows off, citing exhaustion during Moulin Rouge!
items.summary_auto: Megan Thee Stallion was rushed to the hospital after "feeling very ill" while onstage on Broadway in Moulin Rouge! The Musical. She later took to social media to explain she had been "pushing myself past my limits lately."

items.title: Artemis II astronauts lift off for NASA's 10-day mission around the moon in Canadian first
Hershey says it will go back to classic recipe for all Reese's products after criticism
items.summary_auto: Hershey said Wednesday it will use classic recipes for all Reese's products starting next year, a change that comes after the grandson of Reese's founder criticized the company for shifting to cheaper ingredients.

items.title: Canadian music producer Cirkut reflects on Grammy, Juno wins
items.summary_auto: Music producer Henry Walter, known professionally as Cirkut, spent several pivotal teenage years living in Halifax. The acclaimed producer has had a huge year, winning awards at both the Grammys and Junos, so what's next for him?

items.title: First Nations chiefs demand the PM apologize after he said he could 'outlast' protesters
items.summary_auto: Two First Nations chiefs are calling on Prime Minister Mark Carney to apologize for saying he could "outlast" protesters. Chrissy Isaacs, a Grassy Narrows woman suffering from mercury poisoning, was in Toronto on Monday to demand compensation from the provincial government for mercury contamination in her community.
